description: Lineage-head receipt golden vector (RFC-ACDP-0011 §5). A head receipt over the sig-001 golden lineage (version 1 is the only — and therefore head — version), signed by the SAME Ed25519 registry test keypair as rcpt-001 (RFC-ACDP-0011 §5 reuses the RFC-ACDP-0010 receipt signing key and construction verbatim): pins the head-receipt preimage canonical bytes, the receipt hash, and the signature. This is the rcpt-001-equivalent for the serve-time layer — every head-receipt-capable SDK pins it. Executed arithmetically by scripts/conformance-runner.py. Test keypairs MUST NOT be used in production. Values generated by temp/gen-0.3.0-vectors.py.

registry_test_keypair.warning: TEST ONLY. The private seed is publicly known (identical to rcpt-001's). Never use this keypair for real receipts.

vectors.name: Head receipt for the sig-001 golden lineage (v1 head, active), Ed25519-signed
verification_steps: 1. JCS-canonicalize receipt_unsigned (the receipt minus 'signature'); result MUST equal expected.canonical_form.
2. SHA-256 of the canonical bytes; 'sha256:' + hex MUST equal expected.receipt_hash.
3. Sign the ASCII bytes of expected.receipt_hash with the registry test private key; base64 MUST equal expected.signature_value_base64.
4. Verify the signature with the registry test public key; verification MUST succeed.

5. Cross-checks (RFC-ACDP-0011 §7): receipt_version MUST be 'acdp-lhr/1'; the registry_did authority MUST equal the head_ctx_id authority; head_version is 1, so lineage_id MUST equal 'lin:sha256:' + SHA-256(head_ctx_id) — the sig-001 lineage derivation; head_status is 'active' (never 'superseded'); as_of MUST match the canonical millisecond RFC 3339 form.
